subtopics.title: Binary Numbers and Conversion
common_misconceptions: Students often confuse binary place values with decimal place values
Some forget that binary only uses digits 0 and 1
subtopics.teaching_note: Use physical counters to demonstrate the concept of place value in different bases.

subtopics.title: Octal and Hexadecimal Systems
common_misconceptions: Students sometimes confuse hexadecimal letters A-F with variable symbols in algebra
subtopics.teaching_note: Connect octal and hexadecimal to their use in computer science.

subtopics.title: Expanding and Simplifying Brackets
common_misconceptions: Forgetting to multiply every term inside the bracket by the factor outside
Sign errors when expanding negative coefficients
subtopics.teaching_note: Use the area model to visualize expansion. Emphasize the distributive property systematically.

subtopics.title: Factorization Techniques
common_misconceptions: Difficulty identifying common factors in complex expressions
Confusing factorization with expansion
subtopics.teaching_note: Start with numerical examples to establish the concept of common factors.

subtopics.title: Laws of Indices
common_misconceptions: Adding indices when multiplying same bases
Confusing negative indices with negative numbers
subtopics.teaching_note: Build the laws of indices from numerical examples using pattern recognition.

subtopics.title: Introduction to Logarithms
common_misconceptions: Confusing logarithms with algebraic multiplication
Difficulty understanding that logarithm is an exponent
subtopics.teaching_note: Emphasize that logarithms are exponents. Use the equivalence between exponential and logarithmic forms.

subtopics.title: Solving Quadratic Equations by Factorization
common_misconceptions: Setting each factor equal to the number on the right side instead of zero
subtopics.teaching_note: Emphasize that the equation must equal zero before factorizing.

subtopics.title: The Quadratic Formula and Completing the Square
common_misconceptions: Sign errors when identifying coefficients a, b, and c
Forgetting that the discriminant determines the nature of roots
subtopics.teaching_note: Derive the quadratic formula from completing the square. Use the discriminant to predict root nature.

subtopics.title: Angles and Lines
common_misconceptions: Confusing alternate and corresponding angles
subtopics.teaching_note: Use parallel lines cut by a transversal to demonstrate all angle relationships.

subtopics.title: Polygons and Their Properties
common_misconceptions: Using the wrong formula for sum of interior angles
subtopics.teaching_note: Derive the formula (n-2)×180° through dividing polygons into triangles.

subtopics.title: Trigonometric Ratios
common_misconceptions: Confusing sine and cosine ratios
Using trigonometry in non-right-angled triangles
subtopics.teaching_note: Use SOH CAH TOA as a mnemonic. Emphasize that trigonometric ratios only apply to right-angled triangles.

subtopics.title: Measures of Central Tendency
common_misconceptions: Confusing mean with median in skewed distributions
Forgetting to arrange data in order before finding median
subtopics.teaching_note: Use real-life data. Discuss when each measure is most appropriate.

subtopics.title: Measures of Dispersion
common_misconceptions: Confusing variance with standard deviation
subtopics.teaching_note: Emphasize that standard deviation is the square root of variance.

subtopics.title: Set Operations and Venn Diagrams
common_misconceptions: Confusing union with intersection
Forgetting that n(A∪B) = n(A) + n(B) - n(A∩B)
subtopics.teaching_note: Use Venn diagrams as a visual tool. Solve word problems using inclusion-exclusion.

subtopics.title: Direct and Inverse Variation
common_misconceptions: Confusing direct and inverse variation
subtopics.teaching_note: Use real-world examples like speed and time (inverse) or cost and quantity (direct).
